dashboard.title: Migration Progress
dashboard.subtitle: A plain-language view of the current migration run.
language.label: Language
run.label: Run
wave.label: wave
human.working.title: The migration is moving forward
human.working.summary: The harness is reading the current state, performing one bounded action, and validating the result before it advances.
human.working.reason: This is normal guarded execution, not a failure.
human.continuing.title: The migrator is continuing automatically
human.continuing.summary: A safe next action exists, so continuous mode should execute it without asking you to type continue again.
human.continuing.reason: Each action is still reviewed and checked before the next cycle starts.
human.ticket.title: The migrator is improving the current wave
human.ticket.summary: A focused ticket is active. The agent is correcting mappings, evidence, or verification without widening the migration scope.
human.ticket.reason: Small tickets keep changes understandable and make regressions easier to detect.
human.quality.title: The migration works, but this wave needs cleanup
human.quality.summary: Draft Playwright output exists. The next wave is paused while the harness reduces TODOs, fallback code, or unmapped targets.
human.quality.reason: The quality gate prevents avoidable problems from being copied into later waves.
human.scopeRepair.title: A full-project rerun was detected inside a small wave
human.scopeRepair.summary: Nothing is lost. The full draft should be preserved separately, then the current wave must be rerun using its bounded input scope.
human.scopeRepair.reason: Wave budgets are meaningful only when each wave contains the tests assigned to it.
human.done.title: The migration checkpoint is complete
human.done.summary: The required checks passed for this stage and the result is ready for review or the next explicitly allowed phase.
human.done.reason: Completed evidence remains available in the run artifacts.
human.stopped.title: The migration stopped for a real blocker
human.stopped.summary: The harness reached a terminal condition that requires a decision, permission, or a fresh bounded run.
human.stopped.reason: The detailed blocker and next action are shown below.
progress.title: Progress
metric.draftCoverage.note: Tests for which migration output has been produced, even if quality work remains.
metric.acceptedProgress.note: Tests in waves that have passed their bounded checks.
metric.tests.note: Draft output compared with the total tests in the wave plan.
now.title: What is happening now
now.automatic: The harness will continue when allowed
now.noTicket: No focused ticket is active yet
now.noNextAction: The next action has not been materialized yet.
waves.title: Migration waves
waves.empty: No wave plan has been materialized yet.
details.title: Details
quality.title: Quality gate
quality.scopeMismatch.explain: The generated output contains more files or tests than the wave plan. Preserve the full rerun separately and rerun the wave-local wrapper.
gates.title: Safety checks
gates.empty: No safety-check results are available yet.
activity.title: Recent activity
activity.empty: No activity events are available yet.
status.label: Status
summary.title: Summary
policy.title: Harness policy checks
events.title: Events
artifacts.title: Artifacts
hint.humanStatus: This headline translates machine statuses into plain language. Attention does not always mean failure: it often means the harness is deliberately improving quality before scaling.
hint.progress: Progress is split into draft coverage and accepted progress. This avoids calling a useful draft a failure while still showing what has passed the gates.
hint.draftCoverage: Draft coverage counts tests for which output exists. It may be high while the current wave still needs mapping or verification work.
hint.acceptedProgress: Accepted progress counts only waves with a receipt whose metrics fingerprint and generated-tree hash still match. A stale or hand-edited status does not unlock scaling.
hint.tests: The numerator is the latest detected draft test count; the denominator comes from the wave plan. A value above the current wave size can reveal an accidental full-project rerun.
hint.now: This section answers two questions: what the migrator is doing now, and what it should do next without asking you for routine input.
hint.currentTask: A bounded ticket is one small, reviewable improvement such as adding a method mapping or repairing wave evidence.
hint.nextStep: The next action is read from continuation-decision.json or wave-quality-budget.json. In continuous mode it should begin automatically unless a terminal blocker exists.
hint.waves: A wave is a small group of tests migrated together. Small waves make failures easier to understand and prevent one bad mapping from affecting the whole project.
hint.details: Technical evidence is kept here so the main screen stays simple. Open these sections when you need exact gate names, counters, or recent events.
hint.quality: The boundary keeps semantic/fallback/TODO/unmapped metrics for diagnosis, but acceptance is decided by generated-code readiness, blocking root patterns, assertion preservation, validation, scope integrity, and a current acceptance receipt.
hint.gates: Safety checks verify that the agent stayed under migration/**, did not weaken policy, and produced the evidence required by the harness.
hint.activity: Recent activity is taken from the append-only harness event log. It helps distinguish real progress from a repeated loop.
process.title: How the migration works
process.plan.title: Plan
process.plan.copy: Split the Selenium suite into small, understandable waves.
process.draft.title: Generate draft
process.draft.copy: Create Playwright code for the current bounded wave.
process.improve.title: Improve quality
process.improve.copy: Resolve mappings, TODOs, fallbacks, and wave-scope issues.
process.verify.title: Verify
process.verify.copy: Run quality, safety, scope, and runtime evidence checks.
process.accept.title: Accept and continue
process.accept.copy: Mark the wave accepted and start the next planned wave.
previews.title: What the migrated tests look like
previews.empty: No generated C# test preview is available for the current wave yet.
hint.process: This is the full migration loop. The highlighted step is what the harness is doing now; completed steps stay green.
hint.process.plan: A wave is a small group of related tests. Planning keeps failures local and progress measurable.
hint.process.draft: Draft output is useful progress, but it is not considered accepted until quality and safety checks pass.
hint.process.improve: This step is normal. The wave manager prioritizes the highest-payoff reusable root pattern, regenerates the same bounded wave, and compares before/after progress before scaling.
hint.process.verify: Verification checks behavior, quality budgets, artifact integrity, and that the agent stayed inside the allowed workspace.
hint.process.accept: Only a current outcome-bound acceptance receipt unlocks the next wave. Fast mode reduces ceremony, not these quality requirements.
hint.previews: Shows short read-only previews of generated Playwright .NET tests from the current wave. They may still contain TODOs while the quality step is active.
human.limit.title: The automatic remediation budget has been reached
human.limit.summary: The migration preserved its progress, but the remaining limitations need a human decision before another autonomous cycle.
human.limit.reason: This is a controlled stop, not lost work.
intervention.none: No user action is needed right now
hint.intervention: Tells you whether the harness can continue safely on its own or has reached a real decision point.
quality.manager.title: Wave manager decision
quality.manager.pending: The measured wave is waiting for a bounded quality/profit decision.
hint.quality.manager: The wave manager may choose the best allowed next action, but it cannot override hard quality gates.
quality.metrics.title: Outcome and diagnostic metrics
quality.metrics.name: Metric
quality.metrics.actions: Reported semantic / syntax fallback / total actions
hint.quality.metrics: Outcome metrics decide readiness. Semantic, syntax-fallback, action and flat TODO counters remain visible for diagnosis and trend analysis, but cannot grant acceptance by themselves.
